name: Wynton Marsalis
abstract: Wynton Learson Marsalis (born October 18, 1961) is an American trumpeter, composer, and music instructor, who is currently the artistic director of Jazz at Lincoln Center. He has been active in promoting classical and jazz music, often to young audiences.

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: You’ve been lately doing some collaborations with Wynton Marsalis... I think it’s from this respect that we just gravitated to a working relationship... I didn’t take that seriously until I opened at the Blue Note with Clark Terry... to do that album that’s now out, called Boppin’ at the Blue Note.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kent Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: Kent has recorded as a leader of his own group and has shared the stage with Nicholas Payton, Wynton Marsalis, Kevin Eubanks...
about_quotes.doc_title: Kent Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 22, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: I enjoyed a lot working with him... I was to play tuba and bass trombone... for a Wynton Marsalis Lincoln Center concert
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: Wynton had commissioned me, he said, “I want you to write a piece, I’m going to commission several people.” ... We put on hard hats and we went in this place when it was construction workers here... Wynton invites me and... I played a concert at Alice Tully Hall with the Lincoln Center Jazz Orchestr
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Then you recorded with quite a few other people, most recently, in recent years, Wynton Marsalis. ... [INTERVIEWER] But you played a nice single-string on The Death of Jazz on Majesty of the Blues with Wynton Marsalis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Danny Barker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wycliffe Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Wynton for almost, since ’89, eleven, going on twelve years... I was the seventh member to join the septet... I was with Wynton Marsalis. This is a great musical situation.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wycliffe Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001
